name: The Go Engineer v3 — Migration from v2

architectural_decision.decision: Break the locked v2 curriculum and rebuild around the Report 1 phase-based zero-to-software-engineer path.
architectural_decision.v2_policy: curriculum.v2.json is frozen legacy import data, not the active source of truth.
architectural_decision.core_rule: Keep good lessons, break the old path, optimize for learner progression, proof, documentation quality, and job readiness.
architectural_decision.full_bundle_policy: Split files are authoritative. curriculum.v3.full.generated.json is an optional generated snapshot for review/export only.

migration.default_policy: Every legacy item must be mapped to keep, rewrite, split, merge, move-to-elective, replace, or archive. Every migrated README must also receive a legacy_readme_action: reuse, rewrite, split, merge, or archive.

high_confidence_moves.reason: Protobuf/gRPC is valuable but should not block the core beginner path.
Complex generics and generic data structures are advanced.
Functional options are useful but not core beginner material.
sync.Map is specialized and should follow normal map+mutex reasoning.
Blue/green deployment is advanced deployment strategy.
Testing and debugging must move before backend work.
Database path must become SQL/Postgres-first with migrations and EXPLAIN.
Security needs authn/authz strategy and secure API practice.
Helm belongs in optional deployment electives after core containerization and CI/CD.
Kubernetes probes are operationally important but should not block the core path.
Horizontal pod autoscaling is an advanced orchestration topic.
Service mesh concepts are advanced distributed-systems operations topics.

mappings.reason: Duplicate career-readiness content was merged into canonical portfolio/interview lessons in module 16.
Kubernetes and service-mesh topics are valuable but advanced; they no longer block core delivery mastery.
The delivery module was normalized to a clean 19-lesson sequence.
The performance module was normalized after being split from observability.
The observability module was normalized after performance lessons moved to module 13.

readme_migration_policy.reuse: Only allowed when the old README already satisfies the v3 contract after generated navigation updates.
readme_migration_policy.rewrite: Use when the topic is valid but explanation depth, examples, or structure are not world-class.
readme_migration_policy.split: Use when a v2 README teaches multiple v3 lessons.
readme_migration_policy.merge: Use when multiple v2 READMEs become one v3 lesson or project page.
readme_migration_policy.archive: Use when the content is obsolete or moved fully to electives.
